channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: IndiaMART: Scaling Business with Robust Technology
item.description: IndiaMART's success story, scaling business with robust technology, AI-driven marketplace, and customer-centric strategy
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk extends political tentacles into UK, Germany
item.description: Elon Musk is seeking to wield the power of his social media network to boost far-right political movements in the United Kingdom and Germany, after becoming a major backer of President-elect Trump in November’s election.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the Meta glasses the New Orleans attacker used to scout the French Quarter
item.description: The FBI says the New Orleans truck attacker who killed 14 people had previously scouted the French Quarter and recorded video with his Meta smart glasses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: My robot teacher: The challenge of AI in computer science education
item.description: Over the past two years, generative AI has been a force for transformation—and disruption—everywhere it’s landed. Education was no exception; if anything, schools were among the first institutions to grapple with AI’s implications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Find out who’s behind any phone number with this free lookup tool
item.description: I don’t know about you, but practically every time my phone rings, my heart rate starts skyrocketing.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 hidden reasons you keep running out of iCloud storage
item.description: Apple gives every iCloud user 5GB of free storage space. This storage space can be used for anything—from backing up your iPhone online to saving documents in the cloud to syncing photos across devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s Siri settlement feeds the ‘eavesdropping iPhone’ narrative
item.description: Apple, which has built its brand on data privacy, settled a class action suit this week in which the plaintiffs claimed their iPhones’ Siri voice assistant listened in on their conversations for the purpose of targeting them with ads.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Dating Wrapped: TikTok users are crunching the numbers on their dating life
item.description: If Spotify Wrapped left you underwhelmed this year, TikTok’s “Dating Wrapped” trend is here to spice things up. Instead of playlists, users are serving up PowerPoint-style presentations packed with stats about their 2024 dating lives.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Apple TV+ is offering a free weekend of binge-watching
item.description: Apple TV+ is hoping people will make a dent in the Strategic Popcorn Reserve by bingeing its streaming TV and movies for free this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China rolls out its crime-fighting ball to chase down criminals
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a spherical, 276-pound law enforcement robot that has been chasing down criminals at impressive speeds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry
item.description: Our government needs to maintain trust through competence, and that competence will increasingly be about powering government services with AI. The post The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: The biggest Wi-Fi mistake you didn’t know you were making
item.description: There are many untapped safeguards and tips you can learn to take your Wi-Fi knowledge to the next level, technology expert Kim Komando shares her tricks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
